expectations.evidence: Slot 1 lists '`TrodesPosV1`: 1 new row, same PK, computed against the new params blob.' Anti-pattern section explicitly warns: 'Do not `update1` the `trodes_pos_params_name` field on the existing `TrodesPosSelection` row. That mutates the PK in place...'

expectations.text: Identifies that inserting into `PositionOutput.TrodesPosV1` issues a new merge_id on `PositionOutput`
expectations.evidence: Slot 1 #4: '`PositionOutput.TrodesPosV1` + `PositionOutput`: 1 new merge entry with a brand-new UUID `merge_id`. ... `TrodesPosV1.make()` calls `PositionOutput._merge_insert([orig_key], part_name=self.camel_name, skip_duplicates=True)` at `position/v1/position_trodes_position.py:241`, and `_merge_insert` mints a UUID.' (line ref 241 verified correct).

expectations.text: Lists at least two downstream tables that would diverge (linearization, decoding via PositionGroup, ripple via pos_merge_id)
expectations.evidence: Slot 2 table lists FOUR downstream pipelines: Linearization v1 (`LinearizedPositionV1`), Ripple detection v1 (`RippleTimesV1` with `pos_merge_id` PK ref to `ripple/v1/ripple.py:186` — verified), MUA detection v1 (`MuaEventsV1` ref to `mua/v1/mua.py:67` — verified), and Decoding v1 (`*SelectionV1.Position` part with `decoding/v1/core.py:142` — verified).

expectations.text: Explicitly names LFP, spike-sorting, and curation as unaffected — they do not consume position
expectations.evidence: Slot 3 explicitly lists '**LFP / LFPBand**: not on the position branch at all. `LFPV1`, `LFPBandV1`, `LFPOutput`, `LFPBandSelection` are unaffected.' and '**Spike sorting**: `SpikeSortingRecording`, `SpikeSorting`/`SpikeSortingV1`, `CurationV1`, ... entirely upstream/orthogonal to position. No re-sorting.'

expectations.text: Does not use the v0 field name `position_info_param_name` or the nonexistent class `SpikeSortingV1`
expectations.evidence: v0 field name `position_info_param_name` is correctly absent (uses `trodes_pos_params_name` throughout). However the response DOES write `SpikeSorting`/`SpikeSortingV1` in the spike-sorting unaffected list. `class SpikeSortingV1` does not exist in spyglass.spikesorting.v1.sorting (the class is `SpikeSorting`), so this is the nonexistent-class case the expectation forbids.

claims.claim: LinearizationSelection PK includes pos_merge_id at linearization/v1/main.py:103
RippleTimesV1 takes pos_merge_id in its PK at ripple/v1/ripple.py:186
Decoding selection part table .Position has the pos_merge_id FK at decoding/v1/core.py:142
claims.evidence: Line 142: '-> PositionOutput.proj(pos_merge_id=\'merge_id\')' confirmed inside the Position part class.

claims.claim: RippleLFPSelection does NOT depend on position
claims.evidence: Definition of RippleTimesV1 only adds the position FK at the v1 ripple times level (line 186), not on RippleLFPSelection — RippleTimesV1 is the lowest table that introduces pos_merge_id, so RippleLFPSelection is genuinely position-free.

claims.claim: SpikeSortingV1 is a real class in spyglass
claims.evidence: grep 'class SpikeSortingV1' across spyglass/src/spyglass returns no matches. spikesorting/v1/sorting.py defines `class SpikeSorting(SpyglassMixin, dj.Computed)` (line 234) — there is no `SpikeSortingV1` class. The response's slash notation 'SpikeSorting`/`SpikeSortingV1' is misleading; only the former exists.

position_trodes_position.py line 55 declares 'trodes_pos_params_name: varchar(80)' as part of the PK in TrodesPosParams; TrodesPosSelection inherits the file/interval keys from upstream and adds the params name.

suggestions.assertion: Response does NOT contain the substring `SpikeSortingV1`.
suggestions.reason: This forbidden-substring assertion correctly caught a real bug — the response named a nonexistent class. Good discriminator. Worth keeping but consider also adding a positive check that the response uses `SpikeSorting` (the real v1 class name) when listing unaffected branches, so a response that simply omits the spike-sorting line entirely doesn't pass by silent-elision.

No assertion checks that the response correctly distinguishes that `TrodesPosSelection` PK includes `trodes_pos_params_name` (so changing the name yields a NEW row at a NEW PK rather than an update to the existing row). The 'new TrodesPosV1 row produced' assertion is upstream of that PK fact and could pass via hand-waving. A targeted assertion like 'Response identifies that trodes_pos_params_name is part of TrodesPosSelection's PK' would tighten this.

Decoding-pipeline cascade is checked only via the loose 'at least two downstream tables' assertion. The hard part of this counterfactual — that the *Position* part of `*SelectionV1` (not the master) holds the pos_merge_id FK, and that `PositionGroup` itself is untouched — is not asserted directly. A response could pass by listing 'decoding' generically without distinguishing master vs part vs PositionGroup.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions caught the real defect (nonexistent SpikeSortingV1 class). The forbidden-substring check on `SpikeSortingV1` is well-targeted. The positive content assertions are mostly substring presence and could be tightened around the precise mechanism (merge part vs master, PK structure) since this is a hard counterfactual where surface-level fluency could mask conceptual errors.
